name: Pate a La Rapure (Grated Pie)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chicken into individual pieces.
2. Place the chicken in a saucepan and cover with cold water. Bring to a boil.
3. Add onions, celery, carrot, thyme (or 1 bay leaf), salt, and pepper to taste.
4. Cover and simmer for 1½ to 2 hours or until the chicken is tender.
5. Peel and grate the potatoes over a bowl of cold water.
6. When the chicken is cooked, squeeze the grated potatoes into a piece of cotton until quite dry.
7. Place in a saucepan. Add as much boiling broth from the chicken as needed to almost cover the potatoes.
8. Stir thoroughly mixed. Salt lightly.
9. Simmer over low heat for about 10 minutes.
10. Grease generously an 8-inch square baking dish.
11. Spread half of the grated potatoes in the bottom of the pan.
12. Bone the hot chicken and spread over the potatoes.
13. Cover with the remaining half of the grated potatoes.
14. Mince one small onion very finely, add ¼ tsp pepper and 2 slices of fat salt pork cut into very small dice.
15. Bake for ½ hour in a 350°F oven or until the top is golden brown and crisp.
16. Serve hot.
from the Acadian section of The Canadiana Cookbook by Mme. Jehane Benoit. To quote the author, "Rapure in French means grated, so either case, rapure or rappie" indicated that fact. A great deal of French and English is mixed together in the Acadian language.
